convs.name: How Anchors Work

disagreements.detail: Only covers messages 0-1; messages 2-5 (the bulk of the single-topic conversation gold spans as 0-5) are left uncovered (TAIL GAP).

agreements: Single chunk matches gold's single-topic verdict
convs.summary: Gold treats the whole conversation as one anchor-mechanics topic (0-5); the candidate identifies the same topic but truncates its chunk at message 1, dropping four substantive messages. Label is accurate but coverage is badly short.

convs.name: Divorce Rights in Mexico

disagreements.detail: Emits one chunk 0-9 where gold finds three distinct topics: divorce consent law (0-3), history of divorce rights (4-7), and the telenovela production dates (8-9).

agreements: Label acknowledges both the divorce and telenovela subjects
convs.summary: Coverage is complete and well-formed, but the candidate collapses three clearly separable topics into a single span. The compound label gestures at the split it failed to make.

convs.name: Fixing JavaScript ES6 Error

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-23 where gold finds twelve distinct npm/git troubleshooting topics.
End anchor is a spliced/chimera UUID (chimera_prefix), not a real message uuid.
Messages 24-29 uncovered (TAIL GAP).

agreements: Opening topic (ES6 transpilation error) correctly identified
convs.summary: Severe under-segmentation: a twelve-topic conversation reduced to one span that also fabricates its end anchor and drops the final six messages. Among the worst cases.

convs.name: First Hold: Climbing Starting Point

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-7 where gold splits into first-hold definition (0-3) and the challenge mechanism (4-7).

agreements: Label captures both the definition and challenge aspects
convs.summary: Complete coverage with a reasonable compound label, but merges gold's two topics into one. A mild under-split on a short conversation.

convs.name: Encoded String Verification

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-8 where gold separates base64 decoding (0-5) from ASCII interpretation (6-8).

agreements: Label names both the decode-verification and ASCII-interpretation phases
convs.summary: Closely related two-phase task collapsed into one span; coverage is complete and the compound label is accurate. A defensible but coarser-than-gold call.

convs.name: Mud versus clay differences

disagreements.detail: End anchor does not resolve to any message (unresolved); the chunk yields no valid span and all 48 messages (gold finds 24 topics) are uncovered.

convs.summary: Effectively no valid segmentation: the lone chunk has an unresolved hallucinated end anchor, leaving the entire 24-topic conversation uncovered. A functional failure.

convs.name: Comparing Microphone Audio

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-3 where gold separates comparison methodology (0-1) from audio analysis software options (2-3).

agreements: Label names both methodology and analysis software
convs.summary: Two adjacent topics merged into one fully-covered span with an accurate compound label. Minor under-split on a 4-message conversation.

convs.name: Avoid Banding in DaVinci.

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-5 where gold finds six topics spanning Resolve banding/editing and a separate nitroglycerin detonation thread.
Messages 6-13 (the entire nitroglycerin/detonation discussion) uncovered (TAIL GAP).

agreements: Label notices the topic crosses into nitroglycerin
convs.summary: Covers only the first half and merges its topics; the entire detonation-temperature thread (6-13) is dropped. The label hints at the shift it failed to segment or cover.

convs.name: Service Table Design for SIMS

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-3 where gold splits the table design (0-1) from the SurrealQL explanation request (2-3).
Says 'inventory graph table' where gold reads it as a service graph; minor topical drift.

agreements: Captures both the table-design and SurrealQL aspects
convs.summary: Complete coverage with a compound label, but merges gold's two topics and slightly mislabels the domain as inventory rather than service. Minor.

convs.name: File Size Creation Date

disagreements.detail: Only covers 0-1; messages 2-19 uncovered (TAIL GAP), dropping seven of gold's eight topics (DNG, RW2, Arca-Swiss, MP4/MOV, Resolve waveform).

agreements: Opening Node.js file-metadata topic correctly identified
convs.summary: Catches only the first topic and abandons the remaining 18 messages spanning seven distinct subjects. Severe under-coverage.

convs.name: New chat

disagreements.detail: Covers 0-2 but gold spans the whole single topic 0-4; messages 3-4 uncovered (TAIL GAP).

convs.summary: Gold treats this as one translation topic across 0-4; the candidate picks the right topic and single-chunk structure but truncates at message 2, dropping the tail.

convs.name: Toyota's Kaizen Culture

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-3 where gold separates the kaizen-culture identification (0-1) from the Lexus LFA influence (2-3).

agreements: Label captures both kaizen culture and the LFA connection
convs.summary: Two related Toyota topics merged into one fully-covered span with an accurate compound label. Minor under-split.

convs.name: if you look at the articles in articles, you might see that they have a lot of c

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-6 where gold finds seven topics across the citation-migration thread.
Messages 7-48 uncovered (TAIL GAP), dropping nearly the entire conversation (Obsidian rendering, footnotes, block references, final migration, email access).

convs.summary: Covers only the first seven of 49 messages and merges those into one span; 42 messages of substantive content are dropped. Severe under-coverage.

convs.name: Comparing Mac computers for local AI performance

disagreements.detail: End anchor does not resolve to any message (unresolved); no valid span produced and all 74 messages (gold finds 21 topics) uncovered.

convs.summary: Effectively no valid segmentation: the single chunk's end anchor is hallucinated and unresolved, leaving the entire 21-topic conversation uncovered. A functional failure, also the slowest run (101s).

convs.name: EDGE Network Rollout Timeline

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-13 where gold finds three topics: EDGE rollout (0-5), modern iPhone vs 2003 networks (6-9), and Wi-Fi popularization (10-13).

agreements: Label spans the rollout and iPhone/Wi-Fi range
convs.summary: Complete coverage but the three distinct topics are collapsed into one span. The label notes both endpoints without segmenting the middle topic.

convs.name: SR-71s never downed

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-19 where gold finds four topics including a clear shift from SR-71 subjects to drifting-vs-grip driving (16-19).
Start anchor is a spliced/chimera UUID (chimera_prefix), not a real message uuid.

agreements: Label enumerates several real subtopics (crashes, range, pilots, drifting)
convs.summary: Spans the whole conversation but merges four gold topics, including an off-subject driving thread, and fabricates its start anchor. The list-style label acknowledges the very topic diversity it failed to split.

convs.name: Policy Document Database Import

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-11 where gold finds six steps: discovery attempt, Drive recheck, filesystem discovery, schema design, table creation, parsing kickoff.

agreements: Label captures the overarching import-workflow theme
convs.summary: Complete coverage but the six-step workflow gold segments is flattened into one span. The single label is a fair umbrella but loses all the step structure.

convs.name: WebSocket Message Queueing Fix

disagreements.detail: Only covers 0-1; messages 2-13 uncovered (TAIL GAP), dropping six of gold's topics (Proxy, event handlers, subscriptions, reconnect, Deno rewrite).

agreements: Label notes the Proxy aspect that appears later
convs.summary: Catches only the first topic and abandons the remaining 12 messages across six subjects. Severe under-coverage despite a label that gestures beyond its span.

convs.name: LUT effects on image appearance

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-7 where gold finds four topics: LUT-as-film-stock, SOOC philosophy, LUT as constitutive variable, and Lumix LiveLUT system.

agreements: Label captures the LUT-on-color and film-roll-analogy themes
convs.summary: Complete coverage but four distinct conceptual topics are merged into one span. The compound label touches two of the four threads.

convs.name: NinjaOne RMM Overview

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-9 where gold finds four topics: RMM background, Gatekeeper blocking, unsigned-installer diagnosis, and the TeamViewer avoidance decision.

agreements: Label captures both the platform overview and the notarization/signing angle
convs.summary: Complete coverage but four topics collapsed into one span. The compound label spans the RMM and notarization ends while losing the middle structure.

convs.name: Telemedicine Opportunity Decision Analysis

disagreements.detail: Only covers 0-1; messages 2-25 uncovered (TAIL GAP), dropping seven of gold's eight topics (pricing, SWOT, launch-failure risk, time conflict, risk taxonomy, scoring, decision matrix).

convs.summary: Catches only the opening framing and abandons 24 of 26 messages spanning a detailed decision analysis. Severe under-coverage.

convs.name: Google Drive Folder Search

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-13 where gold finds seven topics from folder discovery through sales-objection analysis to a WhiteBit Jira ticket search.

agreements: Label spans the Drive-folder-search and Jira-ticket endpoints
convs.summary: Complete coverage but seven distinct investigative topics are flattened into one span. The compound label captures the first and last subjects only.

convs.name: S5IIX High Resolution Mode

disagreements.detail: Only covers 0-1; messages 2-17 uncovered (TAIL GAP), dropping eight of gold's nine topics (astro features, time-lapse, long exposure, infinity focus, remote control, setup checklist).

convs.name: Untitled

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-3 where gold finds five fine-grained topics (mostly stacked on message 2): PDF approval, thesis/audience framing, evidence sources, PowerPoint generation, revised approval.
Generic 'Codex agent history and tool call transcript' label misses the actual deck/Archie subject matter the gold labels capture.

agreements: Single-chunk granularity is defensible since gold's splits are unusually fine and heavily overlapping on one message
convs.summary: Coverage is complete and the gold's hyper-fine five-way split on a 4-message conversation is itself questionable, so single-chunk granularity is partly excusable. However the label is generic and misses the Archie/deck subject the conversation is actually about.

convs.name: Camera Sensor vs. Display

disagreements.detail: One chunk 0-7 where gold finds four topics: sensor-vs-display, Quad Bayer filter, 48MP-to-24MP creation, and 48MP detail for 24MP reconstruction.

agreements: Label enumerates real subtopics (sensor vs display, Quad Bayer, 48MP to 24MP super-resolution)
convs.summary: Complete coverage and the list-style label names three of the four gold topics, but it merges them into a single span rather than segmenting. A coherent but under-split result.
